title: Greedy Algorithms and Exchange Arguments · Execution Trace: Interval Huffman
summary: Allocate rooms, bandwidth, and deadlines under competing local choices. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 interval huffman increment in a greedy-design decision and proof notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply proven greedy structures to interval scheduling and prefix-code construction.
Construct smallest counterexamples for plausible but invalid heuristics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
